title: Ôn thi TN THPT Thành phố Huế - Đề số 9

part1.q:
Read the following leaflet and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 1 to 6.
PROTECT OUR COASTAL HERITAGE!

Are you seeking a meaningful way to contribute to environmental sustainability? Join us in preserving the unique seven-coloured rock beach—one of the most remarkable natural landscapes in the region.
For nearly two years, a local resident has been (1) _______ awareness of environmental protection through his daily voluntary efforts. The beach, (2) _______ a considerable number of visitors each year, is renowned for its diverse stone formations and tranquil atmosphere. Nevertheless, it is increasingly threatened by pollution resulting from both tourism activities and ocean debris.
Having been born and raised in the area, he has developed a profound attachment (3) _______ his homeland and a strong sense of responsibility to safeguard its natural beauty. His routine involves collecting waste along the coastline and retrieving debris from the sea using basic equipment. As a result, more people have become environmentally (4) _______ and are beginning to adopt more responsible behaviours.
You can also make a difference by participating in our (5) _______. Your contribution will help maintain the ecological balance of this scenic site. Please remember (6) _______ waste properly and encourage others to follow this practice.
Together, we can ensure a cleaner and more sustainable future.
Adapted from https://vietnamnews.vn/environment/1779818/coastal-volunteer-fights-pollution-on-rock-beach.html

Mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best arrangement of utterances or sentences to make a meaningful exchange or text in each of the following questions from 7 to 11.
a. SUE: That’s true. Communication skills are becoming increasingly important in the modern workplace.
b. SUE: What skills do you think students should develop for future careers?
c. MARK: I believe critical thinking is one of the most essential skills nowadays.

Mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best arrangement of utterances or sentences to make a meaningful exchange or text in each of the following questions from 7 to 11.
a. Mark: College is so different from high school. I barely have time to rest.
b. Ella: The workload is insane, but at least we have more freedom.
c. Mark: True, but I miss having a structured schedule.

Mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best arrangement of utterances or sentences to make a meaningful exchange or text in each of the following questions from 7 to 11.
a. Olivia: True, but not everyone has access to a good system.
b. Ethan: That’s why small actions matter too - like recycling and saving electricity.
c. Ethan: That’s great! I think switching to public transport is also a big help.
d. Olivia: I try to reduce my carbon footprint by using less plastic.
e. Olivia: If everyone made little changes, it would make a big difference.

Mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best arrangement of utterances or sentences to make a meaningful exchange or text in each of the following questions from 7 to 11.
a. Sophie: I get that, but if used wisely, they can be great learning assistants.
b. Jake: Do you think AI tools are useful for studying?
c. Jake: That’s true, but some students rely on them too much.
d. Jake: I suppose balance is key - learning, not just copying, is what matters.
e. Sophie: Definitely! They help explain concepts and check my writing.

Mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best arrangement of utterances or sentences to make a meaningful exchange or text in each of the following questions from 7 to 11.
a. Governments and international organisations have implemented a range of measures to mitigate its effects, including promoting renewable energy and enforcing stricter environmental regulations.
b. Climate change is widely acknowledged as one of the most critical challenges confronting humanity in the modern era.
c. Consequently, individuals are increasingly encouraged to adopt environmentally responsible behaviours in their daily lives.
d. These initiatives are designed to curb greenhouse gas emissions and minimise long-term environmental damage.
e. Notwithstanding these efforts, the effectiveness of such measures ultimately depends on the level of public awareness and global cooperation.
f. Therefore, a coordinated approach involving governments, businesses, and individuals is essential to address this issue in a sustainable manner.
options: b – a – d – e – c – f
b – d – a – e – c – f
b – a – e – d – c – f
a – b – d – e – c – f

part1.q:
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best answer to each of the questions from 12 to 21.
The Fundamentals and History of Printmaking
Printmaking is the generic term for a number of processes, of which woodcut and engraving are two prime examples. Prints are made by pressing a sheet of paper (or other material) against an image-bearing surface to which ink has been applied. When the paper is removed, the image adheres to it, but in reverse.
The woodcut had been used in China from the fifth century A.D. for applying patterns to textiles. The process was not introduced into Europe until the fourteenth century, first for textile decoration and then for printing on paper. Woodcuts are created by a relief process; first, the artist takes a block of wood, which has been sawed parallel to the grain, covers it with a white ground, and then draws the image in ink. The background is carved away, leaving the design area slightly raised. The woodblock is inked, and the ink adheres to the raised image. It is then transferred to damp paper either by hand or with a printing press.
Engraving, which grew out of the goldsmith's art, originated in Germany and northern Italy in the middle of the fifteenth century. It is an intaglio process (from Italian intagliare, "to carve"). [I] The image is incised into a highly polished metal plate, usually copper, with a cutting instrument, or burin. The artist inks the plate and wipes it clean so that some ink remains in the incised grooves. An impression is made on damp paper in a printing press, with sufficient pressure being applied so that the paper picks up the ink.
Both woodcut and engraving have distinctive characteristics. [II] Engraving lends itself to subtle modeling and shading through the use of fine lines. Hatching and cross-hatching determine the degree of light and shade in a print. Woodcuts tend to be more linear, with sharper contrasts between light and dark. [III] Printmaking is well suited to the production of multiple images. A set of multiples is called an edition. Both methods can yield several hundred good-quality prints before the original block or plate begins to show signs of wear. [IV] Mass production of prints in the sixteenth century made images available, at a lower cost, to a much broader public than before.
(Adapted from TOEFL Reading Practice: Art and History)
According to paragraph 1, what happens when the paper is removed from the surface?
options: The paper absorbs all the ink evenly
The image sticks to the paper in reverse
The ink remains on the surface only

Which of the following best summarises paragraph 2?
options: A comparative analysis of similarities and differences between woodcut and engraving methods
The historical origins and main procedural steps involved in woodcut printing techniques
An overview of materials commonly used in engraving and their practical artistic applications
The gradual development of printing technology and equipment over different historical periods

According to the passage, all of the following are true about prints EXCEPT that they_____ .
options: may be produced on surfaces besides paper
can display contrasts between brightness and darkness
are transferred directly without reversing the image
may be reproduced multiple times from one original surface

Which of the following best paraphrases the sentence in paragraph 4?
options: Such prints may show some contrast between lighter and darker areas, though not strongly
This method is known for its delicate gradation and finely controlled tonal variation
The process tends to produce smooth transitions and softly blended visual effects
Woodcut prints are characterised by bold outlines and clearly defined differences in brightness

According to the passage, what do woodcut and engraving have in common?
options: They achieve contrast through hatching and cross-hatching.
They allow multiple copies to be produced from one original.
They were first used in Europe.

Which of the following can be inferred from the passage?
options: The woodcut technique is generally more complicated than the engraving method
Engraving eventually replaced woodcut as the dominant printing technique
Printmaking was mainly restricted to professional artists and not widely accessible
The development of printmaking enabled a wider audience to access visual materials

part1.q:
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best answer to each of the questions from 12 to 21.

Woodcuts are created by a relief process; first, the artist takes a block of wood, which has been sawed parallel to the grain, covers it with a white ground, and then draws the image in ink. The background is carved away, leaving the design area slightly raised. The woodblock is inked, and the ink adheres to the raised image. It is then transferred to damp paper either by hand or with a printing press.
Engraving, which grew out of the goldsmith's art, originated in Germany and northern Italy in the middle of the fifteenth century. It is an intaglio process (from Italian intagliare, "to carve"). [I] The image is incised into a highly polished metal plate, usually copper, with a cutting instrument, or burin. The artist inks the plate and wipes it clean so that some ink remains in the incised grooves. An impression is made on damp paper in a printing press, with sufficient pressure being applied so that the paper picks up the ink.
Both woodcut and engraving have distinctive characteristics. [II] Engraving lends itself to subtle modeling and shading through the use of fine lines. Hatching and cross-hatching determine the degree of light and shade in a print. Woodcuts tend to be more linear, with sharper contrasts between light and dark. [III] Printmaking is well suited to the production of multiple images. A set of multiples is called an edition. Both methods can yield several hundred good-quality prints before the original block or plate begins to show signs of wear. [IV] Mass production of prints in the sixteenth century made images available, at a lower cost, to a much broader public than before.
(Adapted from TOEFL Reading Practice: Art and History)
Where in the passage does the following sentence best fit?
“These differences make each method suitable for different artistic purposes.”

Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best answer to each of the questions from 12 to 21.
The Fundamentals and History of Printmaking
Printmaking is the generic term for a number of processes, of which woodcut and engraving are two prime examples. Prints are made by pressing a sheet of paper (or other material) against an image-bearing surface to which ink has been applied. When the paper is removed, the image adheres to it, but in reverse.
The woodcut had been used in China from the fifth century A.D. for applying patterns to textiles. The process was not introduced into Europe until the fourteenth century, first for textile decoration and then for printing on paper. Woodcuts are created by a relief process; first, the artist takes a block of wood, which has been sawed parallel to the grain, covers it with a white ground, and then draws the image in ink. The background is carved away, leaving the design area slightly raised. The woodblock is inked, and the ink adheres to the raised image. It is then transferred to damp paper either by hand or with a printing press.
Engraving, which grew out of the goldsmith's art, originated in Germany and northern Italy in the middle of the fifteenth century. It is an intaglio process (from Italian intagliare, "to carve"). [I] The image is incised into a highly polished metal plate, usually copper, with a cutting instrument, or burin. The artist inks the plate and wipes it clean so that some ink remains in the incised grooves. An impression is made on damp paper in a printing press, with sufficient pressure being applied so that the paper picks up the ink.
Both woodcut and engraving have distinctive characteristics. [II] Engraving lends itself to subtle modeling and shading through the use of fine lines. Hatching and cross-hatching determine the degree of light and shade in a print. Woodcuts tend to be more linear, with sharper contrasts between light and dark. [III] Printmaking is well suited to the production of multiple images. A set of multiples is called an edition. Both methods can yield several hundred good-quality prints before the original block or plate begins to show signs of wear. [IV] Mass production of prints in the sixteenth century made images available, at a lower cost, to a much broader public than before.
(Adapted from TOEFL Reading Practice: Art and History)
Which of the following best summarises the passage?
options: A detailed account of the evolution of European artistic movements and their influence on modern art
A comparison between traditional painting methods and modern printing techniques in visual arts
A comprehensive discussion of various artistic materials and their roles in different visual art forms
An explanation of the main printmaking techniques, including their processes, characteristics, and historical development

part1.q:
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best answer to each of the numbered questions from 22 to 29 .
From Bone to Rubber: The Progress of False Teeth
For centuries, people have searched for a way to replace dead and decaying teeth with comfortable false teeth. Many materials have been used to make a set of false teeth. The teeth themselves should be made from a hard and durable material. They should be secured to a soft material, making them easy to wear. In the last two decades, dentists succeeded in making durable false teeth that are comfortable, too.
Two thousand years ago, the Etruscans made teeth out of animal bone and gold. These materials were used-with varying degrees of success-up to the 1700’s. When George Washington was president, ivory from animals such as elephants became a popular material for false teeth. Doctors and inventors also tried silver, pearl, and agate, but teeth made from these materials were very expensive. Perhaps the most successful material was porcelain, invented by a Frenchman about two hundred years ago. White, strong, and resistant to decay, porcelain is still used today for making single teeth.
Besides finding a material for the teeth, inventors also had to find a way to secure them in a person’s mouth. People tried wire, springs, and many kinds of glue to accomplish this. In most cases, however, discomfort and a likelihood of the teeth falling out plagued the person who wore them.
Around 1844, an American dentist named Horace Wells used laughing gas to put people to sleep before working on their teeth. This innovation made dental work a lot less painful. Soon after, an inventor created the first form of rubber. This was important to dentistry because teeth could be attached to the rubber, and the rubber could be molded to fit the shape of the mouth. With these two developments, dentist could work without causing pain and could fit teeth more carefully. False teeth have become more available and comfortable since then, and dentists have continued to improve the making and use of false teeth.
(Adapted from Reading Comprehension Skills: Levels of Reading Ability)
Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as a requirement for false teeth?
options: The teeth must be made from durable materials.
Their cost should be affordable for most people.
Wearing them should be comfortable for users.
A soft base is needed to support the teeth.

Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 30 to 34.
Bánh đúc is a traditional Vietnamese dish renowned for its smooth texture and subtle flavour. Among its various adaptations, bánh đúc riêu cua stands out due to its distinctive taste and intricate preparation process. Its appealing flavour largely results from the careful combination of ingredients and cooking techniques involved in its preparation. (30) _______. Although it is prepared from relatively simple ingredients such as freshwater crabs, tomatoes and rice flour, the cooking procedure requires considerable precision.
The crabs are crushed and filtered to extract a clear liquid, which is subsequently heated until it forms delicate clusters of crab roe. The tomatoes are sautéed with shallots to intensify the flavour. (31) _______. This broth is then poured over portions of steamed rice cake.
The dish is typically served with fresh herbs and vegetables, enhancing both its nutritional value and visual appeal. There is a small eatery on Láng Hạ Street (32) _______. It has gained a loyal customer base over the years, despite operating only on specific days.
(33) ________, the rice cake is sliced into small portions or elongated strands depending on preference. This stage plays a crucial role in achieving the desired texture. Many diners appreciate the dish not only for its taste but also for its cultural significance.
(34) _______. Visitors to Bắc Ninh may also have the opportunity to experience this speciality alongside traditional folk performances.
options: Therefore, the dish requires careful preparation to achieve its rich and balanced flavour.
In contrast, the dish is now associated with several modern international cooking styles.
Consequently, many diners consider the dish less memorable than other local specialities.
As a result, some restaurants simplify the cooking process to reduce preparation time.

part1.q:
Read the following leaflet and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 35 to 40.
ESTABLISHING AN EFFECTIVE ENGLISH CLUB
Are you seeking to enhance your communicative competence through meaningful interaction? Establishing an English club can serve as an effective platform for engaging in authentic language use and developing long-term proficiency.
English clubs operate in less formal settings; (35) ________, they enable learners to communicate with greater confidence and spontaneity. Some participants favour structured discussions, while (36) ________ are more inclined towards interactive activities such as simulations or role-playing. This diversity fosters sustained engagement.
A well-functioning club can be maintained (37) ________ limited resources, provided that members demonstrate commitment and collaboration. With (38) ________ organisation, participants are more likely to sustain regular attendance and achieve consistent progress. In addition, the selection of an appropriate venue is essential (39) ________ accessibility has a direct impact on participation rates.
The (40) ________ of participants tends to increase when the club is systematically managed, thereby enhancing its overall effectiveness. Therefore, involvement in such initiatives is highly recommended for learners aiming to refine their communicative abilities.
Adapted from https://learningenglish.voanews.com/a/learning-english-with-english-clubs-starting-and-organizing-your-club/3947928.html

Read the following leaflet and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 35 to 40.
ESTABLISHING AN EFFECTIVE ENGLISH CLUB
Are you seeking to enhance your communicative competence through meaningful interaction? Establishing an English club can serve as an effective platform for engaging in authentic language use and developing long-term proficiency.
English clubs operate in less formal settings; (35) ________, they enable learners to communicate with greater confidence and spontaneity. Some participants favour structured discussions, while (36) ________ are more inclined towards interactive activities such as simulations or role-playing. This diversity fosters sustained engagement.
A well-functioning club can be maintained (37) ________ limited resources, provided that members demonstrate commitment and collaboration. With (38) ________ organisation, participants are more likely to sustain regular attendance and achieve consistent progress. In addition, the selection of an appropriate venue is essential (39) ________ accessibility has a direct impact on participation rates.
The (40) ________ of participants tends to increase when the club is systematically managed, thereby enhancing its overall effectiveness. Therefore, involvement in such initiatives is highly recommended for learners aiming to refine their communicative abilities.
Adapted from https://learningenglish.voanews.com/a/learning-english-with-english-clubs-starting-and-organizing-your-club/3947928.html
